title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn, Chapters 01 to 05
summaries: "Adventures of Huckleberry Finn, Chapters 01 to 05" by Mark Twain is a novel first published in 1884-1885. This sequel to "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer" follows young Huck Finn as he escapes his abusive father and embarks on a daring journey down the Mississippi River. Accompanied by Jim, a runaway slave seeking freedom, Huck confronts difficult moral choices in a society steeped in racism. Written entirely in vernacular English, this groundbreaking work combines adventure, satire, and a deeply felt portrayal of boyhood in antebellum Missouri.
